translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forasmuch as many have undertaken to draw up a relation concerning the matters fully believed among us,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as those who from the beginning were eye-witnesses of and attendants on the Word have delivered them to us,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: it has seemed good to me also, accurately acquainted from the origin with all things, to write to thee with method, most excellent Theophilus,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that thou mightest know the certainty of those things in which thou hast been instructed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judaea, a certain priest, by name Zacharias, of the course of Abia, and his wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name Elizabeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were both just beforeGod, walking in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord blameless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both advanced in years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as he fulfilled his priestly service beforeGod in the order of his course,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: it fell to him by lot, according to the custom of the priesthood, to enter into the temple of the Lord to burn incense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the multitude of the people were praying without at the hour of incense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And an angel of [the] Lord appeared to him, standing on the right of the altar of incense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zacharias was troubled, seeing [him], and fear fell upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the angel said to him, Fear not, Zacharias, because thy supplication has been heard, and thy wife Elizabeth shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name John.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall be to thee joy and rejoicing, and many shall rejoice at his birth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he shall be great before [the] Lord, and he shall drink no wine nor strong drink; and he shall be filled with [the] Holy Spirit, even from his mother's womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And many of the sons of Israel shall he turn to [the] Lord theirGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall go before him in [the] spirit and power of Elias, to turn hearts of fathers to children, and disobedient ones to [the] thoughts of just [men], to make ready for [the] Lord a prepared people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zacharias said to the angel, How shall I know this, for I am an old man, and my wife advanced in years?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel answering, said to him, I am Gabriel, who stand beforeGod, and I have been sent to speak to thee, and to bring these glad tidings to thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and behold, thou shalt be silent and not able to speak, till the day in which these things shall take place, because thou hast not believed my words, the which shall be fulfilled in their time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people were awaiting Zacharias, and they wondered at his delaying in the temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when he came out he could not speak to them, and they recognised that he had seen a vision in the temple. And he was making signs to them, and continued dumb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when the days of his service were completed, he departed to his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now after these days, Elizabeth his wife conceived, and hid herself five months, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus has [the] Lord done to me in [these] days in which he looked upon [me] to take away my reproach among men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in the sixth month, the angel Gabriel was sent ofGod to a city of Galilee, of which [the] name [was] Nazareth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to a virgin betrothed to a man whose name [was] Joseph, of the house of David; and the virgin's name [was] Mary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel came in to her, and said, Hail, [thou] favoured one! the Lord [is] with thee: [blessed art thou amongst women].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she, [seeing] [the angel], was troubled at his word, and reasoned in her mind what this salutation might be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel said to her, Fear not, Mary, for thou hast found favour withGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and behold, thou shalt conceive in the womb and bear a son, and thou shalt call his name Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall be great, and shall be called Son of [the] Highest; and [the] LordGod shall give him the throne of David his father;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he shall reign over the house of Jacob for the ages, and of his kingdom there shall not be an end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Mary said to the angel, How shall this be, since I know not a man?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel answering said to her, [The] Holy Spirit shall come upon thee, and power of [the] Highest overshadow thee, wherefore the holy thing also which shall be born shall be called Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, Elizabeth, thy kinswoman, she also has conceived a son in her old age, and this is the sixth month to her that was called barren:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for nothing shall be impossible withGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Mary said, Behold the bondmaid of [the] Lord; be it to me according to thy word. And the angel departed from her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Mary, rising up in those days, went into the hill country with haste, to a city of Judah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and entered into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elizabeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as Elizabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leaped in her womb; and Elizabeth was filled with [the] Holy Spirit,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and cried out with a loud voice and said, Blessed [art] thou amongst women, and blessed the fruit of thy womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whence [is] this to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For behold, as the voice of thy salutation sounded in my ears, the babe leaped with joy in my womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And blessed [is] she that has believed, for there shall be a fulfilment of the things spoken to her from [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Mary said, My soul magnifies the Lord,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and my spirit has rejoiced inGod my Saviour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he has looked upon the low estate of his bondmaid; for behold, from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the Mighty One has done to me great things, and holy [is] his name;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his mercy [is] to generations and generations to them that fear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He has wrought strength with his arm; he has scattered haughty [ones] in the thought of their heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He has put down rulers from thrones, and exalted the lowly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He has filled the hungry with good things, and sent away the rich empty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He has helped Israel his servant, in order to remember mercy,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (as he spoke to our fathers,) to Abraham and to his seed for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Mary abode with her about three months, and returned to her house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the time was fulfilled for Elizabeth that she should bring forth, and she gave birth to a son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And her neighbours and kinsfolk heard that [the] Lord had magnified his mercy with her, and they rejoiced with her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child, and they called it after the name of his father, Zacharias.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his mother answering said, No; but he shall be called John.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to her, There is no one among thy kinsfolk who is called by this name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made signs to his father as to what he might wish it to be called.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having asked for a writing-table, he wrote saying, John is his name. And they all wondered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue, and he spake, blessingGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And fear came upon all who dwelt round about them; and in the whole hill-country of Judaea all these things were the subject of conversation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all who heard them laid them up in their heart, saying, What then will this child be? And [the] Lord's hand was with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zacharias his father was filled with [the] Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be [the] Lord theGod of Israel, because he has visited and wrought redemption for his people,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and raised up a horn of deliverance for us in the house of David his servant;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as he spoke by [the] mouth of his holy prophets, who have been since the world began;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: deliverance from our enemies and out of the hand of all who hate us;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to fulfil mercy with our fathers and remember his holy covenant,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [the] oath which he swore to Abraham our father,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to give us, that, saved out of the hand of our enemies, we should serve him without fear

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in piety and righteousness before him all our days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, child, shalt be called [the] prophet of [the] Highest; for thou shalt go before the face of [the] Lord to make ready his ways;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to give knowledge of deliverance to his people by [the] remission of their sins

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: on account of [the] bowels of mercy of ourGod; wherein [the] dayspring from on high has visited us,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to shine upon them who were sitting in darkness and in [the] shadow of death, to guide our feet into [the] way of peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the child grew and was strengthened in spirit; and he was in the deserts until the day of his shewing to Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But it came to pass in those days that a decree went out from Caesar Augustus, that a census should be made of all the habitable world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The census itself first took place when Cyrenius had the government of Syria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all went to be inscribed in the census roll, each to his own city:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Joseph also went up from Galilee out of the city Nazareth to Judaea, to David's city, the which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to be inscribed in the census roll with Mary who was betrothed to him [as his] wife, she being great with child.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, while they were there, the days of her giving birth [to her child] were fulfilled,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and she brought forth her first-born son, and wrapped him up in swaddling-clothes and laid him in the manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were shepherds in that country abiding without, and keeping watch by night over their flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, an angel of [the] Lord was there by them, and [the] glory of [the] Lord shone around them, and they feared [with] great fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel said to them, Fear not, for behold, I announce to you glad tidings of great joy, which shall be to all the people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for to-day a Saviour has been born to you in David's city, who is Christ [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the sign to you: ye shall find a babe wrapped in swaddling-clothes, and lying in a manger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host, praisingGod and saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Glory toGod in the highest, and on earth peace, good pleasure in men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as the angels departed from them into heaven, that the shepherds said to one another, Let us make our way then now as far as Bethlehem, and let us see this thing that is come to pass, which the Lord has made known to us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came with haste, and found both Mary and Joseph, and the babe lying in the manger;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having seen [it] they made known about the country the thing which had been said to them concerning this child.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all who heard [it] wondered at the things said to them by the shepherds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Mary kept all these things [in her mind], pondering [them] in her heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praisingGod for all things which they had heard and seen, as it had been said to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when eight days were fulfilled for circumcising him, his name was called Jesus, which was the name given by the angel before he had been conceived in the womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the days were fulfilled for their purifying according to the law of Moses, they brought him to Jerusalem to present [him] to the Lord

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (as it is written in the law of [the] Lord: Every male that opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to offer a sacrifice according to what is said in the law of [the] Lord: A pair of turtle doves, or two young pigeons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon; and this man was just and pious, awaiting the consolation of Israel, and [the] Holy Spirit was upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was divinely communicated to him by the Holy Spirit, that he should not see death before he should see [the] Lord's Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came in the Spirit into the temple; and as the parents brought in the child Jesus that they might do for him according to the custom of the law,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he received him into his arms, and blessedGod, and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lord, now thou lettest thy bondman go, according to thy word, in peace;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for mine eyes have seen thy salvation,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which thou hast prepared before the face of all peoples;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a light for revelation of [the] Gentiles and [the] glory of thy people Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his father and mother wondered at the things which were said concerning him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary his mother, Lo, this [child] is set for the fall and rising up of many in Israel, and for a sign spoken against;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (and even a sword shall go through thine own soul;) so that [the] thoughts may be revealed from many hearts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a prophetess, Anna, daughter of Phanuel, of [the] tribe of Asher, who was far advanced in years, having lived with [her] husband seven years from her virginity,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and herself a widow up to eighty-four years; who did not depart from the temple, serving night and day with fastings and prayers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and she coming up the same hour gave praise to the Lord, and spoke of him to all those who waited for redemption in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had completed all things according to the law of [the] Lord, they returned to Galilee to their own city Nazareth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the child grew and waxed strong [in spirit], filled with wisdom, andGod's grace was upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his parents went yearly to Jerusalem at the feast of the passover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was twelve years old, and they went up [to Jerusalem] according to the custom of the feast

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and had completed the days, as they returned, the boy Jesus remained behind in Jerusalem, and his parents knew not [of it];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but, supposing him to be in the company that journeyed together, they went a day's journey, and sought him among their relations and acquaintances:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and not having found him they returned to Jerusalem seeking him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, after three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers and hearing them and asking them questions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all who heard him were astonished at his understanding and answers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they saw him they were amazed: and his mother said to him, Child, why hast thou dealt thus with us? behold, thy father and I have sought thee distressed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Why [is it] that ye have sought me? did ye not know that I ought to be [occupied] in my Father's business?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they understood not the thing that he said to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went down with them and came to Nazareth, and he was in subjection to them. And his mother kept all these things in her heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus advanced in wisdom and stature, and in favour withGod and men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now in the fifteenth year of the government of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judaea, and Herod tetrarch of Galilee, and Philip his brother tetrarch of Ituraea and the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, [the] word ofGod came upon John, the son of Zacharias, in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came into all the district round the Jordan, preaching [the] baptism of repentance for [the] remission of sins,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as it is written in [the] book of [the] words of Esaias the prophet: Voice of one crying in the wilderness: Prepare ye the way of [the] Lord, make straight his paths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every gorge shall be filled up, and every mountain and hill shall be brought low, and the crooked [places] shall become a straight [path], and the rough places smooth ways,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all flesh shall see the salvation ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He said therefore to the crowds which went out to be baptised by him, Offspring of vipers, who has forewarned you to flee from the coming wrath?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Produce therefore fruits worthy of repentance; and begin not to say in yourselves, We have Abraham for [our] father, for I say unto you thatGod is able of these stones to raise up children to Abraham.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And already also the axe is applied to the root of the trees; every tree therefore not producing good fruit is cut down and cast into [the] fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the crowds asked him saying, What should we do then?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering says to them, He that has two body-coats, let him give to him that has none; and he that has food, let him do likewise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And tax-gatherers came also to be baptised, and they said to him, Teacher, what should we do?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Take no more [money] than what is appointed to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And persons engaged in military service also asked him saying, And we, what should we do? And he said to them, Oppress no one, nor accuse falsely, and be satisfied with your pay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as the people were in expectation, and all were reasoning in their hearts concerning John whether he might be the Christ,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: John answered all, saying, I indeed baptise you with water, but the mightier than I is coming, the thong of whose sandals I am not fit to unloose; he shall baptise you with [the] Holy Spirit and fire;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whose winnowing-fan is in his hand, and he will thoroughly purge his threshing-floor, and will gather the wheat into his garner, but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Exhorting then many other things also he announced [his] glad tidings to the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him as to Herodias, the wife of his brother, and as to all the wicked things which Herod had done,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: added this also to all [the rest], that he shut up John in prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, all the people having been baptised, and Jesus having been baptised and praying, that the heaven was opened,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the Holy Spirit descended in a bodily form as a dove upon him; and a voice came out of heaven, Thou art my beloved Son, in thee I have found my delight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus himself was beginning to be about thirty years old; being as was supposed son of Joseph; of Eli,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Matthat, of Levi, of Melchi, of Janna, of Joseph,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Mattathias, of Amos, of Naoum, of Esli, of Naggai,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Maath, of Mattathias, of Semei, of Joseph, of Juda,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Joannes, of Resa, of Zorobabel, of Salathiel, of Neri,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Melchi, of Addi, of Cosam, of Elmodam, of Er,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Joses, of Eliezer, of Joreim, of Matthat, of Levi,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Simeon, of Juda, of Joseph, of Jonan, of Eliakim,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Meleas, of Menan, of Mattatha, of Nathan, of David,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Jesse, of Obed, of Booz, of Salmon, of Naasson,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Aminadab, of Aram, of Esrom, of Phares, of Juda,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Jacob, of Isaac, of Abraham, of Terah, of Nachor,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Seruch, of Ragau, of Phalek, of Eber, of Sala,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Cainan, of Arphaxad, of Sem, of Noe, of Lamech,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Methusala, of Enoch, of Jared, of Maleleel, of Cainan,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of Enos, of Seth, of Adam, ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, full of [the] Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led by the Spirit in the wilderness

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: forty days, tempted of the devil; and in those days he did not eat anything, and when they were finished he hungered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the devil said to him, If thou be Son ofGod, speak to this stone, that it become bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answered unto him saying, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [the devil], leading him up into a high mountain, shewed him all the kingdoms of the habitable world in a moment of time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the devil said to him, I will give thee all this power, and their glory; for it is given up to me, and to whomsoever I will I give it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore thou wilt do homage before me, all [of it] shall be thine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering him said, It is written, Thou shalt do homage to [the] Lord thyGod, and him alone shalt thou serve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he led him to Jerusalem, and set him on the edge of the temple, and said to him, If thou be Son ofGod, cast thyself down hence;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for it is written, He shall give charge to his angels concerning thee to keep thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and on [their] hands shall they bear thee, lest in any wise thou strike thy foot against a stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to him, It is said, Thou shalt not tempt [the] Lord thyGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the devil, having completed every temptation, departed from him for a time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee; and a rumour went out into the whole surrounding country about him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he taught in their synagogues, being glorified of all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to Nazareth, where he was brought up; and he entered, according to his custom, into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and stood up to read.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [the] book of the prophet Esaias was given to him; and having unrolled the book he found the place where it was written,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [The] Spirit of [the] Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to preach glad tidings to [the] poor; he has sent me to preach to captives deliverance, and to [the] blind sight, to send forth [the] crushed delivered,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to preach [the] acceptable year of [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having rolled up the book, when he had delivered it up to the attendant, he sat down; and the eyes of all in the synagogue were fixed upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he began to say to them, To-day this scripture is fulfilled in your ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all bore witness to him, and wondered at the words of grace which were coming out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this the son of Joseph?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Ye will surely say to me this parable, Physician, heal thyself; whatsoever we have heard has taken place in Capernaum do here also in thine own country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Verily I say to you, that no prophet is acceptable in his [own] country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But of a truth I say to you, There were many widows in Israel in the days of Elias, when the heaven was shut up for three years and six months, so that a great famine came upon all the land,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to none of them was Elias sent but to Sarepta of Sidonia, to a woman [that was] a widow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, and none of them was cleansed but Naaman the Syrian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were all filled with rage in the synagogue, hearing these things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and rising up they cast him forth out of the city, and led him up to the brow of the mountain upon which their city was built, so that they might throw him down the precipice;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he, passing through the midst of them, went his way,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and descended to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and taught them on the sabbaths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were astonished at his doctrine, for his word was with authority.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was in the synagogue a man having a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried with a loud voice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Eh! what have we to do with thee, Jesus, Nazarene? hast thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy [One] ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out from him. And the demon, having thrown him down into the midst, came out from him without doing him any injury.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And astonishment came upon all, and they spoke to one another, saying, What word [is] this? for with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a rumour went out into every place of the country round concerning him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And rising up out of the synagogue, he entered into the house of Simon. But Simon's mother-in-law was suffering under a bad fever; and they asked him for her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And standing over her, he rebuked the fever, and it left her; and forthwith standing up she served them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the sun went down, all, as many as had persons sick with divers diseases, brought them to him, and having laid his hands on every one of them, he healed them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and demons also went out from many, crying out and saying, Thou art the Son ofGod. And rebuking them, he suffered them not to speak, because they knew him to be the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was day he went out, and went into a desert place, and the crowds sought after him, and came up to him, and [would have] kept him back that he should not go from them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said to them, I must needs announce the glad tidings of the kingdom ofGod to the other cities also, for for this I have been sent forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as the crowd pressed on him to hear the word ofGod, that he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he saw two ships standing by the lake, but the fishermen, having come down from them, were washing their nets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And getting into one of the ships, which was Simon's, he asked him to draw out a little from the land; and he sat down and taught the crowds out of the ship.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when he ceased speaking, he said to Simon, Draw out into the deep [water] and let down your nets for a haul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Simon answering said to him, Master, having laboured through the whole night we have taken nothing, but at thy word I will let down the net.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having done this, they enclosed a great multitude of fishes. And their net broke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they beckoned to their partners who were in the other ship to come and help them, and they came, and filled both the ships, so that they were sinking.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Simon Peter, seeing it, fell at Jesus' knees, saying, Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For astonishment had laid hold on him, and on all those who were with him, at the haul of fishes which they had taken;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in like manner also on James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. And Jesus said to Simon, Fear not; henceforth thou shalt be catching men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having run the ships on shore, leaving all they followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he was in one of the cities, that behold, there was a man full of leprosy, and seeing Jesus, falling upon his face, he besought him saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou art able to cleanse me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And stretching forth his hand he touched him, saying, I will; be thou cleansed: and immediately the leprosy departed from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he enjoined him to tell no one; but go, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing as Moses ordained, for a testimony to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the report concerning him was spread abroad still more, and great crowds came together to hear, and to be healed from their infirmities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he withdrew himself, and was about in the desert [places] and praying.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on one of the days, that he was teaching, and there were Pharisees and doctors of the law sitting by, who were come out of every village of Galilee and Judaea and [out of] Jerusalem; and [the] Lord's power was [there] to heal them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, men bringing upon a couch a man who was paralysed; and they sought to bring him in, and put [him] before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And not finding what way to bring him in, on account of the crowd, going up on the housetop they let him down through the tiles, with his little couch, into the midst before Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seeing their faith, he said, Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason [in their minds], saying, Who is this who speaks blasphemies? Who is able to forgive sins butGod alone?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, knowing their reasonings, answering said to them, Why reason ye in your hearts?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which is easier, to say, Thy sins are forgiven thee; or to say, Rise up and walk?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that ye may know that the Son of man has power on earth to forgive sins, he said to the paralysed man, I say to thee, Arise, and take up thy little couch and go to thine house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately standing up before them, having taken up that whereon he was laid, he departed to his house, glorifyingGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And astonishment seized all, and they glorifiedGod, and were filled with fear, saying, We have seen strange things to-day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after these things he went forth and saw a tax-gatherer, Levi by name, sitting at the receipt of taxes, and said to him, Follow me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having left all, rising up, he followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Levi made a great entertainment for him in his house, and there was a great crowd of tax-gatherers and others who were at table with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their scribes and the Pharisees murmured at his disciples, saying, Why do ye eat and drink with tax-gatherers and sinners?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, They that are in sound health have not need of a physician, but those that are ill.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am not come to call righteous [persons], but sinful [ones] to repentance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, Why do the disciples of John fast often and make supplications, in like manner those also of the Pharisees, but thine eat and drink?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Can ye make the sons of the bridechamber fast when the bridegroom is with them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But days will come when also the bridegroom will have been taken away from them; then shall they fast in those days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke also a parable to them: No one puts a piece of a new garment upon an old garment, otherwise he will both rend the new, and the piece which is from the new will not suit with the old.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And no one puts new wine into old skins, otherwise the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be poured out, and the skins will be destroyed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but new wine is to be put into new skins, and both are preserved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And no one having drunk old wine [straightway] wishes for new, for he says, The old is better.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on [the] second-first sabbath, that he went through cornfields, and his disciples were plucking the ears and eating [them], rubbing [them] in their hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But some of the Pharisees said to them, Why do ye what is not lawful to do on the sabbath?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, Have ye not read so much as this, what David did when he hungered, he and those who were with him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: how he entered into the house ofGod and took the shewbread and ate, and gave to those also who were with him, which it is not lawful that [any] eat, unless the priests alone?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, The Son of man is Lord of the sabbath also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on another sabbath also that he entered into the synagogue and taught; and there was a man there, and his right hand was withered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes and the Pharisees were watching if he would heal on the sabbath, that they might find something of which to accuse him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man who had the withered hand, Get up, and stand in the midst. And having risen up he stood [there].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to them, I will ask you if it is lawful on the sabbath to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy [it]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having looked around on them all, he said to him, Stretch out thy hand. And he did [so] and his hand was restored as the other.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they were filled with madness, and they spoke together among themselves what they should do to Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in those days that he went out into the mountain to pray, and he spent the night in prayer toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was day he called his disciples, and having chosen out twelve from them, whom also he named apostles:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon, to whom also he gave the name of Peter, and Andrew his brother, [and] James and John, [and] Philip and Bartholomew,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [and] Matthew and Thomas, James the [son] of Alphaeus and Simon who was called Zealot,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [and] Judas [brother] of James, and Judas Iscariote, who was also [his] betrayer;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having descended with them, he stood on a level place, and a crowd of his disciples, and a great multitude of the people from all Judaea and Jerusalem, and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear him, and to be healed of their diseases;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and those that were beset by unclean spirits were healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the crowd sought to touch him, for power went out from him and healed all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he, lifting up his eyes upon his disciples, said, Blessed [are] ye poor, for yours is the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed ye that hunger now, for ye shall be filled. Blessed ye that weep now, for ye shall laugh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed are ye when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you [from them], and shall reproach [you], and cast out your name as wicked, for the Son of man's sake:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: rejoice in that day and leap for joy, for behold, your reward is great in the heaven, for after this manner did their fathers act toward the prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But woe to you rich, for ye have received your consolation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to you that are filled, for ye shall hunger. Woe to you who laugh now, for ye shall mourn and weep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe, when all men speak well of you, for after this manner did their fathers to the false prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But to you that hear I say, Love your enemies; do good to those that hate you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: bless those that curse you; pray for those who use you despitefully.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To him that smites thee on the cheek, offer also the other; and from him that would take away thy garment, forbid not thy body-coat also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To every one that asks of thee, give; and from him that takes away what is thine, ask it not back.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as ye wish that men should do to you, do ye also to them in like manner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if ye love those that love you, what thank is it to you? for even sinners love those that love them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if ye do good to those that do good to you, what thank is it to you? for even sinners do the same.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if ye lend to those from whom ye hope to receive, what thank is it to you? [for] even sinners lend to sinners that they may receive the like.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing in return, and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be sons of [the] Highest; for he is good to the unthankful and wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be ye therefore merciful, even as your Father also is merciful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And judge not, and ye shall not be judged; condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned. Remit, and it shall be remitted to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give, and it shall be given to you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall be given into your bosom: for with the same measure with which ye mete it shall be measured to you again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke also a parable to them: Can a blind [man] lead a blind [man]? shall not both fall into [the] ditch?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The disciple is not above his teacher, but every one that is perfected shall be as his teacher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But why lookest thou on the mote which is in the eye of thy brother, but perceivest not the beam which is in thine own eye?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: or how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, allow [me], I will cast out the mote that is in thine eye, thyself not seeing the beam that is in thine eye? Hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine eye, and then thou shalt see clear to cast out the mote which is in the eye of thy brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there is no good tree which produces corrupt fruit, nor a corrupt tree which produces good fruit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for every tree is known by its own fruit, for figs are not gathered from thorns, nor grapes vintaged from a bramble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The good man, out of the good treasure of his heart, brings forth good; and the wicked [man] out of the wicked, brings forth what is wicked: for out of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things that I say?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every one that comes to me, and hears my words and does them, I will shew you to whom he is like.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is like a man building a house, who dug and went deep, and laid a foundation on the rock; but a great rain coming, the stream broke upon that house, and could not shake it, for it had been founded on the rock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that has heard and not done, is like a man who has built a house on the ground without [a] foundation, on which the stream broke, and immediately it fell, and the breach of that house was great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he had completed all his words in the hearing of the people, he entered into Capernaum.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain centurion's bondman who was dear to him was ill and about to die;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having heard of Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, begging him that he might come and save his bondman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, being come to Jesus, besought him diligently, saying, He is worthy to whom thou shouldest grant this,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he loves our nation, and himself has built the synagogue for us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus went with them. But already, when he was not far from the house, the centurion sent to him friends, saying to him, Lord, do not trouble thyself, for I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore neither did I count myself worthy to come to thee. But say by a word and my servant shall be healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers, and I say to this [one], Go, and he goes; and to another, Come, and he comes; and to my bondman, Do this, and he does [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus hearing this wondered at him, and turning to the crowd following him said, I say to you, Not even in Israel have I found so great faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they who had been sent returning to the house found the bondman, who was ill, in good health.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass afterwards he went into a city called Nain, and many of his disciples and a great crowd went with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he drew near to the gate of the city, behold, a dead man was carried out, the only son of his mother, and she a widow, and a very considerable crowd of the city [was] with her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord, seeing her, was moved with compassion for her, and said to her, Weep not;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and coming up he touched the bier, and the bearers stopped. And he said, Youth, I say to thee, Wake up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the dead sat up and began to speak; and he gave him to his mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And fear seized on all, and they glorifiedGod, saying, A great prophet has been raised up amongst us; andGod has visited his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this report went out in all Judaea concerning him, and in all the surrounding country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples of John brought him word concerning all these things:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and John, having called two of his disciples, sent to Jesus, saying, Art thou he that is coming, or are we to wait for another?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the men having come to him said, John the baptist has sent us to thee, saying, Art thou he that is coming, or are we to wait for another?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In that hour he healed many of diseases and plagues and evil spirits, and to many blind he granted sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, Go, bring back word to John of what ye have seen and heard: that blind see, lame walk, lepers are cleansed, deaf hear, dead are raised, poor are evangelized;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and blessed is whosoever shall not be offended in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the messengers of John having departed, he began to speak to the crowds concerning John: What went ye out into the wilderness to behold? a reed shaken by the wind?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But what went ye out to see? a man clothed in delicate garments? Behold, those who are in splendid clothing and live luxuriously are in the courts of kings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But what went ye out to see? a prophet? Yea, I say to you, and [what is] more excellent than a prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is he concerning whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I say unto you, Among them that are born of women a greater [prophet] is no one than John [the baptist]; but he who is a little one in the kingdom ofGod is greater than he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (And all the people who heard [it], and the tax-gatherers, justifiedGod, having been baptised with the baptism of John;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the Pharisees and the lawyers rendered null as to themselves the counsel ofGod, not having been baptised by him.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To whom therefore shall I liken the men of this generation, and to whom are they like?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are like children sitting in the market-place, and calling one to another and saying, We have piped to you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For John the baptist has come neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and ye say, He has a demon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Son of man has come eating and drinking, and ye say, Behold an eater and wine-drinker, a friend of tax-gatherers and sinners;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and wisdom has been justified of all her children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But one of the Pharisees begged him that he would eat with him. And entering into the house of the Pharisee he took his place at table;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and behold, a woman in the city, who was a sinner, and knew that he was sitting at meat in the house of the Pharisee, having taken an alabaster box of myrrh,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and standing at his feet behind [him] weeping, began to wash his feet with tears; and she wiped them with the hairs of her head, and kissed his feet, and anointed [them] with the myrrh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisee who had invited him, seeing it, spoke with himself saying, This [person] if he were a prophet would have known who and what the woman is who touches him, for she is a sinner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to him, Simon, I have somewhat to say to thee. And he says, Teacher, say [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There were two debtors of a certain creditor: one owed five hundred denarii and the other fifty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but as they had nothing to pay, he forgave both of them [their debt]: [say,] which of them therefore will love him most?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Simon answering said, I suppose he to whom he forgave the most. And he said to him, Thou hast rightly judged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And turning to the woman he said to Simon, Seest thou this woman? I entered into thy house; thou gavest me not water on my feet, but she has washed my feet with tears, and wiped them with her hair.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou gavest me not a kiss, but she from the time I came in has not ceased kissing my feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My head with oil thou didst not anoint, but she has anointed my feet with myrrh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For which cause I say to thee, Her many sins are forgiven; for she loved much; but he to whom little is forgiven loves little.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, Thy sins are forgiven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that were with [them] at table began to say within themselves, Who is this who forgives also sins?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to the woman, Thy faith has saved thee; go in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass afterwards that he went through [the country] city by city, and village by village, preaching and announcing the glad tidings of the kingdom ofGod; and the twelve [were] with him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and certain women who had been healed of wicked spirits and infirmities, Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Joanna, wife of Chuza, Herod's steward, and Susanna, and many others, who ministered to him of their substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a great crowd coming together, and those who were coming to him out of each city, he spoke by parable:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sower went out to sow his seed; and as he sowed, some fell along the way, and it was trodden under foot, and the birds of the heaven devoured it up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and other fell upon the rock, and having sprung up, it was dried up because it had not moisture;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and other fell in the midst of the thorns, and the thorns having sprung up with [it] choked it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and other fell into the good ground, and having sprung up bore fruit a hundredfold. As he said these things he cried, He that has ears to hear, let him hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples asked him [saying], What may this parable be?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, To you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom ofGod, but to the rest in parables, in order that seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the parable is this: The seed is the word ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But those by the wayside are those who hear; then comes the devil and takes away the word from their heart that they may not believe and be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But those upon the rock, those who when they hear receive the word with joy; and these have no root, who believe for a time, and in time of trial fall away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that that fell where the thorns were, these are they who having heard go away and are choked under cares and riches and pleasures of life, and bring no fruit to perfection.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that in the good ground, these are they who in an honest and good heart, having heard the word keep it, and bring forth fruit with patience.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And no one having lighted a lamp covers it with a vessel or puts it under a couch, but sets it on a lamp-stand, that they who enter in may see the light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there is nothing hid which shall not become manifest, nor secret which shall not be known and come to light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed therefore how ye hear; for whosoever has, to him shall be given, and whosoever has not, even what he seems to have shall be taken from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his mother and his brethren came to him, and could not get to him because of the crowd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told him [saying], Thy mother and thy brethren stand without, wishing to see thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said to them, My mother and my brethren are those who hear the word ofGod and do [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on one of the days, that he entered into a ship, himself and his disciples; and he said to them, Let us pass over to the other side of the lake; and they set off from shore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they sailed, he fell asleep; and a sudden squall of wind came down on the lake, and they were filled [with water], and were in danger;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and coming to [him] they woke him up, saying, Master, master, we perish. But he, rising up, rebuked the wind and the raging of the water; and they ceased, and there was a calm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Where is your faith? And, being afraid, they were astonished, saying to one another, Who then is this, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they arrived in the country of the Gadarenes, which is over against Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he got out [of the ship] on the land, a certain man out of the city met him, who had demons a long time, and put on no clothes, and did not abide in a house, but in the tombs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But seeing Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus Son of the Most HighGod? I beseech thee torment me not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he had commanded the unclean spirit to go out from the man. For very often it had seized him; and he had been bound, kept with chains and fetters; and breaking the bonds he was driven by the demon into the deserts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus asked him saying, What is thy name? And he said, Legion: for many demons had entered into him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they besought him that he would not command them to go away into the bottomless pit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was there a herd of many swine feeding on the mountain, and they besought him that he would suffer them to enter into those; and he suffered them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the demons, going out from the man, entered into the swine, and the herd rushed down the precipice into the lake, and were choked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they that fed [them], seeing what had happened, fled, and told [it] to the city and to the country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went out to see what had happened, and came to Jesus, and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting, clothed and sensible, at the feet of Jesus. And they were afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they also who had seen it told them how the possessed man had been healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the multitude of the surrounding country of the Gadarenes asked him to depart from them, for they were possessed with great fear; and he, entering into the ship, returned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the man out of whom the demons had gone besought him that he might be with him. But he sent him away, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Return to thine house and relate how great thingsGod has done for thee. And he went away through the whole city, publishing how great things Jesus had done for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jesus returned, the crowd received him gladly, for they were all expecting him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, a man came, whose name was Jairus, and he was [a] ruler of the synagogue, and falling at the feet of Jesus besought him to come to his house,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, and she was dying. And as he went the crowds thronged him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a woman who had a flux of blood since twelve years, who, having spent all her living on physicians, could not be cured by any one,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: coming up behind, touched the hem of his garment, and immediately her flux of blood stopped.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said, Who has touched me? But all denying, Peter and those with him said, Master, the crowds close thee in and press upon thee, and sayest thou, Who has touched me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said, Some one has touched me, for I have known that power has gone out from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman, seeing that she was not hid, came trembling, and falling down before him declared before all the people for what cause she had touched him, and how she was immediately healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, [Be of good courage,] daughter; thy faith has healed thee; go in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While he was yet speaking, comes some one from the ruler of the synagogue, saying to him, Thy daughter is dead; do not trouble the teacher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, hearing it, answered him saying, Fear not: only believe, and she shall be made well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he came to the house he suffered no one to go in but Peter and John and James and the father of the child and the mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all were weeping and lamenting her. But he said, Do not weep, for she has not died, but sleeps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they derided him, knowing that she had died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, having turned them all out and taking hold of her hand, cried saying, Child, arise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And her spirit returned, and immediately she rose up; and he commanded [something] to eat to be given to her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And her parents were amazed; but he enjoined them to tell no one what had happened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called together the twelve, he gave them power and authority over all demons, and to heal diseases,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and sent them to proclaim the kingdom ofGod and to heal the sick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Take nothing for the way, neither staff, nor scrip, nor bread, nor money; nor to have two body-coats apiece.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And into whatsoever house ye enter, there abide and thence go forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as many as may not receive you, going forth from that city, shake off even the dust from your feet for a witness against them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going forth they passed through the villages, announcing the glad tidings and healing everywhere.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Herod the tetrarch heard of all the things which were done [by him], and was in perplexity, because it was said by some that John was risen from among [the] dead,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and by some that Elias had appeared, and by others that one of the old prophets had risen again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Herod said, John I have beheaded, but who is this of whom I hear such things? and he sought to see him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the apostles having returned related to him whatever they had done. And he took them and withdrew apart into [a desert place of] a city called Bethsaida.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the crowds knowing [it] followed him; and he received them and spake to them of the kingdom ofGod, and cured those that had need of healing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the day began to decline, and the twelve came and said to him, Send away the crowd that they may go into the villages around, and [into] the fields, and lodge and find victuals, for here we are in a desert place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Give ye them to eat. And they said, We have not more than five loaves and two fishes, unless we should go and buy food for all this people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for they were about five thousand men. And he said to his disciples, Make them sit down in companies by fifties.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they did so, and made them all sit down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And taking the five loaves and the two fishes, looking up to heaven he blessed them, and broke and gave to the disciples to set before the crowd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they all ate and were filled; and there was taken up of what had remained over and above to them in fragments twelve hand-baskets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he was praying alone, his disciples were with him, and he asked them saying, Who do the crowds say that I am?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they answering said, John the baptist; but others, Elias; and others, that one of the old prophets has risen again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, But ye, who do ye say that I am? And Peter answering said, The Christ ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But, earnestly charging them, he enjoined [them] to say this to no man,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, The Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to [them] all, If any one will come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for whosoever shall desire to save his life shall lose it, but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake, he shall save it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what shall a man profit if he shall have gained the whole world, and have destroyed, or come under the penalty of the loss of himself?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whosoever shall have been ashamed of me and of my words, of him will the Son of man be ashamed when he shall come in his glory, and [in that] of the Father, and of the holy angels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you of a truth, There are some of those standing here who shall not taste death until they shall have seen the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after these words, about eight days, that taking Peter and John and James he went up into a mountain to pray.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he prayed the fashion of his countenance became different and his raiment white [and] effulgent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, two men talked with him, who were Moses and Elias,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who, appearing in glory, spoke of his departure which he was about to accomplish in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter and those with him were oppressed with sleep: but having fully awoke up they saw his glory, and the two men who stood with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as they departed from him, Peter said to Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tabernacles, one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias: not knowing what he said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as he was saying these things, there came a cloud and overshadowed them, and they feared as they entered into the cloud:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there was a voice out of the cloud saying, This is my beloved Son: hear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as the voice was [heard] Jesus was found alone: and they kept silence, and told no one in those days any of the things they had seen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the following day, when they came down from the mountain, a great crowd met him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, a man from the crowd cried out saying, Teacher, I beseech thee look upon my son, for he is mine only child:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and behold, a spirit takes him, and suddenly he cries out, and it tears him with foaming, and with difficulty departs from him after crushing him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I besought thy disciples that they might cast him out, and they could not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, O unbelieving and perverted generation, how long shall I be with you and suffer you? Bring hither thy son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as he was yet coming, the demon tore him and dragged him all together. And Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the child and gave him back to his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all were astonished at the glorious greatness ofGod. And as all wondered at all the things which [Jesus] did, he said to his disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye let these words sink into your ears. For the Son of man is about to be delivered into men's hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they understood not this saying, and it was hid from them that they should not perceive it. And they feared to ask him concerning this saying.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a reasoning came in amongst them, who should be [the] greatest of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, seeing the reasoning of their heart, having taken a little child set it by him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to them, Whosoever shall receive this little child in my name receives me, and whosoever shall receive me receives him that sent me. For he who is the least among you all, he is great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And John answering said, Master, we saw some one casting out demons in thy name, and we forbad him, because he follows not with us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, Forbid [him] not, for he that is not against you is for you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the days of his receiving up were fulfilled, that he stedfastly set his face to go to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent messengers before his face. And having gone they entered into a village of the Samaritans that they might make ready for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they did not receive him, because his face was [turned as] going to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples James and John seeing [it] said, Lord, wilt thou that we speak [that] fire come down from heaven and consume them, as also Elias did?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But turning he rebuked them [and said, Ye know not of what spirit ye are].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went to another village.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as they went in the way, one said to him, I will follow thee wheresoever thou goest, Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, The foxes have holes and the birds of the heaven roosting-places, but the Son of man has not where he may lay his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to another, Follow me. But he said, Lord, allow me to go first and bury my father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to him, Suffer the dead to bury their own dead, but do thou go and announce the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And another also said, I will follow thee, Lord, but first allow me to bid adieu to those at my house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to him, No one having laid his hand on [the] plough and looking back is fit for the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now after these things the Lord appointed seventy others also, and sent them two and two before his face into every city and place where he himself was about to come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, The harvest indeed [is] great, but the workmen few; supplicate therefore the Lord of the harvest that he may send out workmen into his harvest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go: behold I send you forth as lambs in the midst of wolves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Carry neither purse nor scrip nor sandals, and salute no one on the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And into whatsoever house ye enter, first say, Peace to this house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
