translations.text: And if a son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon it; but if not it shall turn to you again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the same house abide, eating and drinking such things as they have; for the workman is worthy of his hire. Remove not from house to house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And into whatsoever city ye may enter and they receive you, eat what is set before you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and heal the sick in it, and say to them, The kingdom ofGod is come nigh to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But into whatsoever city ye may have entered and they do not receive you, go out into its streets and say,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even the dust of your city, which cleaves to us on the feet, do we shake off against you; but know this, that the kingdom ofGod is come nigh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I say to you that it shall be more tolerable for Sodom in that day than for that city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to thee, Chorazin! woe to thee, Bethsaida! for if the works of power which have taken place in you had taken place in Tyre and Sidon, they had long ago repented, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Capernaum, who hast been raised up to heaven, shalt be brought down even to hades.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hears you hears me; and he that rejects you rejects me; and he that rejects me rejects him that sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the seventy returned with joy, saying, Lord, even the demons are subject to us through thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, I beheld Satan as lightning falling out of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I give you the power of treading upon serpents and scorpions and over all the power of the enemy, and nothing shall in anywise injure you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subjected to you, but rejoice that your names are written in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the same hour Jesus rejoiced in spirit and said, I praise thee, Father, Lord of the heaven and of the earth, that thou hast hid these things from wise and prudent, and hast revealed them to babes: yea, Father, for thus has it been well-pleasing in thy sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All things have been delivered to me by my Father, and no one knows who the Son is but the Father, and who the Father is but the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son is pleased to reveal [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having turned to the disciples privately he said, Blessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I say to you that many prophets and kings have desired to see the things which ye behold, and did not see [them]; and to hear the things which ye hear, and did not hear [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, a certain lawyer stood up tempting him, and saying, Teacher, having done what, shall I inherit life eternal?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, What is written in the law? how readest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said, Thou shalt love the Lord thyGod with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thine understanding; and thy neighbour as thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Thou hast answered right: this do and thou shalt live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, desirous of justifying himself, said to Jesus, And who is my neighbour?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus replying said, A certain man descended from Jerusalem to Jericho and fell into [the hands of] robbers, who also, having stripped him and inflicted wounds, went away leaving him in a half-dead state.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain priest happened to go down that way, and seeing him, passed on on the opposite side;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in like manner also a Levite, being at the spot, came and looked [at him] and passed on on the opposite side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a certain Samaritan journeying came to him, and seeing [him], was moved with compassion,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and came up [to him] and bound up his wounds, pouring in oil and wine; and having put him on his own beast, took him to [the] inn and took care of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the morrow [as he left], taking out two denarii he gave them to the innkeeper, and said to him, Take care of him, and whatsoever thou shalt expend more, I will render to thee on my coming back.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which [now] of these three seems to thee to have been neighbour of him who fell into [the hands of] the robbers?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, He that shewed him mercy. And Jesus said to him, Go, and do thou likewise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as they went that he entered into a certain village; and a certain woman, Martha by name, received him into her house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she had a sister called Mary, who also, having sat down at the feet of Jesus was listening to his word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Martha was distracted with much serving, and coming up she said, Lord, dost thou not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Speak to her therefore that she may help me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus answering said to her, Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but there is need of one, and Mary has chosen the good part, the which shall not be taken from her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he was in a certain place praying, when he ceased, one of his disciples said to him, Lord, teach us to pray, even as John also taught his disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, When ye pray, say, Father, thy name be hallowed; thy kingdom come;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: give us our needed bread for each day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and remit us our sins, for we also remit to every one indebted to us; and lead us not into temptation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Who among you shall have a friend, and shall go to him at midnight and say to him, Friend, let me have three loaves,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: since a friend of mine on a journey is come to me, and I have nothing to set before him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he within answering should say, Do not disturb me; the door is already shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise up to give [it] thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — I say to you, Although he will not get up and give [them] to him because he is his friend, because of his shamelessness, at any rate, he will rise and give him as many as he wants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I say to you, Ask, and it shall be given to you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every one that asks receives; and he that seeks finds; and to him that knocks it will be opened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But of whom of you that is a father shall a son ask bread, and [the father] shall give him a stone? or also a fish, and instead of a fish shall give him a serpent?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: or if also he shall ask an egg, shall give him a scorpion?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore ye, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much rather shall the Father who is of heaven give [the] Holy Spirit to them that ask him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was casting out a demon, and it was dumb; and it came to pass, the demon being gone out, the dumb [man] spoke. And the crowds wondered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But some from among them said, By Beelzebub the prince of the demons casts he out demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And others tempting [him] sought from him a sign out of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, knowing their thoughts, said to them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation: and a house set against a house falls;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if also Satan is divided against himself, how shall his kingdom subsist? because ye say that I cast out demons by Beelzebub.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if I by Beelzebub cast out demons, your sons — by whom do they cast [them] out? For this reason they shall be your judges.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if by the finger ofGod I cast out demons, then the kingdom ofGod is come upon you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the strong [man] armed keeps his own house, his goods are in peace;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but when the stronger than he coming upon [him] overcomes him, he takes away his panoply in which he trusted, and he will divide the spoil [he has taken] from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is not with me is against me, and he that gathers not with me scatters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the unclean spirit has gone out of the man, he goes through dry places seeking rest; and not finding [any] he says, I will return to my house whence I came out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having come, he finds it swept and adorned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he goes and takes seven other spirits worse than himself, and entering in, they dwell there; and the last condition of that man becomes worse than the first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he spake these things, a certain woman, lifting up her voice out of the crowd, said to him, Blessed is the womb that has borne thee, and the paps which thou hast sucked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said, Yea rather, blessed are they who hear the word ofGod and keep [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as the crowds thronged together, he began to say, This generation is a wicked generation: it seeks a sign, and a sign shall not be given to it but the sign of Jonas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as Jonas was a sign to the Ninevites, thus shall also the Son of man be to this generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and shall condemn them: for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, more than Solomon is here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Men of Nineveh shall stand up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and behold, more than Jonas is here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But no one having lit a lamp sets it in secret, nor under the corn-measure, but on the lamp-stand, that they who enter in may see the light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lamp of the body is thine eye: when thine eye is simple, thy whole body also is light; but when it is wicked, thy body also is dark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore thy whole body [is] light, not having any part dark, it shall be all light as when the lamp lights thee with its brightness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as he spoke, a certain Pharisee asked him that he would dine with him; and entering in he placed himself at table.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Pharisee seeing [it] wondered that he had not first washed before dinner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Lord said to him, Now do ye Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and of the dish, but your inward [parts] are full of plunder and wickedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fools, has not he who has made the outside made the inside also?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But rather give alms of what ye have, and behold, all things are clean to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But woe unto you, Pharisees, for ye pay tithes of mint and rue and every herb, and pass by the judgment and the love ofGod: these ye ought to have done, and not have left those aside.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe unto you, Pharisees, for ye love the first seat in the synagogues and salutations in the market-places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe unto you, for ye are as the sepulchres which appear not, and the men walking over them do not know [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of the doctors of the law answering says to him, Teacher, in saying these things thou insultest us also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, To you also woe, doctors of the law, for ye lay upon men burdens heavy to bear, and yourselves do not touch the burdens with one of your fingers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe unto you, for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, but your fathers killed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye bear witness then, and consent to the works of your fathers; for they killed them, and ye build [their sepulchres].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this reason also the wisdom ofGod has said, I will send to them prophets and apostles, and of these shall they kill and drive out by persecution,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the blood of all the prophets which has been poured out from the foundation of the world may be required of this generation,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zacharias, who perished between the altar and the house; yea, I say to you, it shall be required of this generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe unto you, the doctors of the law, for ye have taken away the key of knowledge; yourselves have not entered in, and those who were entering in ye have hindered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he said these things to them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to press him urgently, and to make him speak of many things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: watching him, [and seeking] to catch something out of his mouth, [that they might accuse him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those [times], the myriads of the crowd being gathered together, so that they trod one on another, he began to say to his disciples first, Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but there is nothing covered up which shall not be revealed, nor secret that shall not be known;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore whatever ye have said in the darkness shall be heard in the light, and what ye have spoken in the ear in chambers shall be proclaimed upon the housetops.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say to you, my friends, Fear not those who kill the body and after this have no more that they can do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I will shew you whom ye shall fear: Fear him who after he has killed has authority to cast into hell; yea, I say to you, Fear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are not five sparrows sold for two assaria? and one of them is not forgotten beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But even the hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore, ye are better than many sparrows.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say to you, Whosoever shall confess me before men, the Son of man will confess him also before the angels ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he that shall have denied me before men shall be denied before the angels ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and whoever shall say a word against the Son of man it shall be forgiven him; but to him that speaks injuriously against the Holy Spirit it shall not be forgiven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when they bring you before the synagogues and rulers and the authorities, be not careful how or what ye shall answer, or what ye shall say;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the Holy Spirit shall teach you in the hour itself what should be said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a person said to him out of the crowd, Teacher, speak to my brother to divide the inheritance with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said to him, Man, who established me [as] a judge or a divider over you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Take heed and keep yourselves from all covetousness, for [it is] not because a man is in abundance [that] his life is in his possessions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke a parable to them, saying, The land of a certain rich man brought forth abundantly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he reasoned within himself saying, What shall I do? for I have not [a place] where I shall lay up my fruits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, This will I do: I will take away my granaries and build greater, and there I will lay up all my produce and my good things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast much good things laid by for many years; repose thyself, eat, drink, be merry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod said to him, Fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee; and whose shall be what thou hast prepared?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus is he who lays up treasure for himself, and is not rich towardGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his disciples, For this cause I say unto you, Be not careful for life, what ye shall eat, nor for the body, what ye shall put on.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The life is more than food, and the body than raiment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Consider the ravens, that they sow not nor reap; which have neither storehouse nor granary; andGod feeds them. How much better are ye than the birds?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But which of you by being careful can add to his stature one cubit?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore ye cannot [do] even what is least, why are ye careful about the rest?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Consider the lilies how they grow: they neither toil nor spin; but I say unto you, Not even Solomon in all his glory was clothed as one of these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ifGod thus clothe the grass, which to-day is in the field and to-morrow is cast into [the] oven, how much rather you, O ye of little faith?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye, seek not what ye shall eat or what ye shall drink, and be not in anxiety;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for all these things do the nations of the world seek after, and your Father knows that ye have need of these things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but seek his kingdom, and [all] these things shall be added to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear not, little flock, for it has been the good pleasure of your Father to give you the kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sell what ye possess and give alms; make to yourselves purses which do not grow old, a treasure which does not fail in the heavens, where thief does not draw near nor moth destroy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For where your treasure is, there also will your heart be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let your loins be girded about, and lamps burning;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye like men who wait their own lord whenever he may leave the wedding, that when he comes and knocks, they may open to him immediately.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed are those bondmen whom the lord [on] coming shall find watching; verily I say unto you, that he will gird himself and make them recline at table, and coming up will serve them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if he come in the second watch, and come in the third watch, and find [them] thus, blessed are those [bondmen].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this know, that if the master of the house had known in what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be dug through.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye therefore, be ye ready, for in the hour in which ye do not think [it], the Son of man comes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter said to him, Lord, sayest thou this parable to us, or also to all?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord said, Who then is the faithful and prudent steward, whom his lord will set over his household, to give the measure of corn in season?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is that bondman whom his lord [on] coming shall find doing thus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: verily I say unto you, that he will set him over all that he has.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if that bondman should say in his heart, My lord delays to come, and begin to beat the menservants and the maidservants, and to eat and to drink and to be drunken,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the lord of that bondman shall come in a day when he does not expect it, and in an hour he knows not of, and shall cut him in two and appoint his portion with the unbelievers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that bondman who knew his own lord's will, and had not prepared [himself] nor done his will, shall be beaten with many [stripes];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he who knew [it] not, and did things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few. And to every one to whom much has been given, much shall be required from him; and to whom [men] have committed much, they will ask from him the more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have come to cast a fire on the earth; and what will I if already it has been kindled?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I have a baptism to be baptised with, and how am I straitened until it shall have been accomplished!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Think ye that I have come to give peace in the earth? Nay, I say to you, but rather division:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for from henceforth there shall be five in one house divided; three shall be divided against two, and two against three:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: father against son, and son against father; mother against daughter, and daughter against mother; a mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said also to the crowds, When ye see a cloud rising out of the west, straightway ye say, A shower is coming; and so it happens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when [ye see] the south wind blow, ye say, There will be heat; and it happens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hypocrites, ye know how to judge of the appearance of the earth and of the heaven; how [is it then that] ye do not discern this time?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And why even of yourselves judge ye not what is right?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as thou goest with thine adverse party before a magistrate, strive in the way to be reconciled with him, lest he drag thee away to the judge, and the judge shall deliver thee to the officer, and the officer cast thee into prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I say unto thee, Thou shalt in no wise come out thence until thou hast paid the very last mite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now at the same time there were present some who told him of the Galileans whose blood Pilate mingled with [that of] their sacrifices.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to them, Think ye that these Galileans were sinners beyond all the Galileans because they suffered such things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No, I say to you, but if ye repent not, ye shall all perish in the same manner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or those eighteen on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them, think ye that they were debtors beyond all the men who dwell in Jerusalem?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No, I say to you, but if ye repent not, ye shall all perish in like manner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke this parable: A certain [man] had a fig-tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit upon it and did not find [any].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to the vinedresser, Behold, [these] three years I come seeking fruit on this fig-tree and find none: cut it down; why does it also render the ground useless?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering says to him, Sir, let it alone for this year also, until I shall dig about it and put dung,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if it shall bear fruit — but if not, after that thou shalt cut it down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was teaching in one of the synagogues on the sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, [there was] a woman having a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and she was bent together and wholly unable to lift her head up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, seeing her, called to [her], and said to her, Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he laid his hands upon her; and immediately she was made straight, and glorifiedGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the ruler of the synagogue, indignant because Jesus healed on the sabbath, answering said to the crowd, There are six days in which [people] ought to work; in these therefore come and be healed, and not on the sabbath day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord therefore answered him and said, Hypocrites! does not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or his ass from the manger and leading [it] away, water [it]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this [woman], who is a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan has bound, lo, [these] eighteen years, ought she not to be loosed from this bond on the sabbath day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he said these things, all who were opposed to him were ashamed; and all the crowd rejoiced at all the glorious things which were being done by him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, To what is the kingdom ofGod like? and to what shall I liken it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is like a grain of mustard [seed] which a man took and cast into his garden; and it grew and became a great tree, and the birds of heaven lodged in its branches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again he said, To what shall I liken the kingdom ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal until the whole was leavened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went through one city and village after another, teaching, and journeying to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one said to him, Sir, [are] such as are to be saved few in number? But he said unto them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Strive with earnestness to enter in through the narrow door, for many, I say to you, will seek to enter in and will not be able.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the time that the master of the house shall have risen up and shall have shut the door, and ye shall begin to stand without and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, open to us; and he answering shall say to you, I know you not whence ye are:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then shall ye begin to say, We have eaten in thy presence and drunk, and thou hast taught in our streets;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he shall say, I tell you, I do not know you whence ye are; depart from me, all [ye] workers of iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth, when ye shall see Abraham and Isaac and Jacob and all the prophets in the kingdom ofGod, but yourselves cast out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall come from east and west, and from north and south, and shall lie down at table in the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there are last who shall be first, and there are first who shall be last.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The same hour certain Pharisees came up, saying to him, Get out, and go hence, for Herod is desirous to kill thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Go, tell that fox, Behold, I cast out demons and accomplish cures to-day and to-morrow, and the third [day] I am perfected;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I must needs walk to-day and to-morrow and the [day] following, for it must not be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the [city] that kills the prophets and stones those that are sent unto her, how often would I have gathered thy children together, as a hen her brood under her wings, and ye would not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, your house is left unto you; and I say unto you, that ye shall not see me until it come that ye say, Blessed [is] he that comes in the name of [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as he went into the house of one of the rulers, [who was] of the Pharisees, to eat bread on [the] sabbath, that they were watching him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there was a certain dropsical [man] before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering spoke unto the doctors of the law and Pharisees, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they were silent. And taking him he healed him and let him go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And answering he said to them, Of which of you shall an ass or ox fall into a well, that he does not straightway pull him up on the sabbath day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were not able to answer him to these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke a parable to those that were invited, remarking how they chose out the first places, saying to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thou art invited by any one to a wedding, do not lay thyself down in the first place at table, lest perhaps a more honourable than thou be invited by him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he who invited thee and him come and say to thee, Give place to this [man], and then thou begin with shame to take the last place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when thou hast been invited, go and put thyself down in the last place, that when he who has invited thee comes, he may say to thee, Friend, go up higher: then shalt thou have honour before all that are lying at table with thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for every one that exalts himself shall be abased, and he that abases himself shall be exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said also to him that had invited him, When thou makest a dinner or a supper, call not thy friends, nor thy brethren, nor thy kinsfolk, nor rich neighbours, lest it may be they also should invite thee in return, and a recompense be made thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when thou makest a feast, call poor, crippled, lame, blind:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt be blessed; for they have not [the means] to recompense thee; for it shall be recompensed thee in the resurrection of the just.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of those that were lying at table with [them], hearing these things, said to him, Blessed [is] he who shall eat bread in the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, A certain man made a great supper and invited many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent his bondman at the hour of supper to say to those who were invited, Come, for already all things are ready.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all began, without exception, to excuse themselves. The first said to him, I have bought land, and I must go out and see it; I pray thee hold me for excused.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to prove them; I pray thee hold me for excused.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And another said, I have married a wife, and on this account I cannot come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the bondman came up and brought back word of these things to his lord. Then the master of the house, in anger, said to his bondman, Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring here the poor and crippled and lame and blind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the bondman said, Sir, it is done as thou hast commanded, and there is still room.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lord said to the bondman, Go out into the ways and fences and compel to come in, that my house may be filled;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I say to you, that not one of those men who were invited shall taste of my supper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And great crowds went with him; and, turning round, he said to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any man come to me, and shall not hate his own father and mother, and wife, and children, and brothers, and sisters, yea, and his own life too, he cannot be my disciple;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and whoever does not carry his cross and come after me cannot be my disciple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For which of you, desirous of building a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost, if he have what [is needed] to complete it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in order that, having laid the foundation of it, and not being able to finish it, all who see it do not begin to mock at him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, This man began to build and was not able to finish?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or what king, going on his way to engage in war with another king, does not, sitting down first, take counsel whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him coming against him with twenty thousand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if not, while he is yet far off, having sent an embassy, he asks for terms of peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus then every one of you who forsakes not all that is his own cannot be my disciple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Salt [then] [is] good, but if the salt also has become savourless, wherewith shall it be seasoned?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is proper neither for land nor for dung; it is cast out. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the tax-gatherers and the sinners were coming near to him to hear him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the Pharisees and the scribes murmured, saying, This [man] receives sinners and eats with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke to them this parable, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What man of you having a hundred sheep, and having lost one of them, does not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness and go after that which is lost, until he find it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having found it, he lays it upon his own shoulders, rejoicing;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and being come to the house, calls together the friends and the neighbours, saying to them, Rejoice with me, for I have found my lost sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I say unto you, that thus there shall be joy in heaven for one repenting sinner, [more] than for ninety and nine righteous who have no need of repentance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or, what woman having ten drachmas, if she lose one drachma, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and seek carefully till she find it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having found it she calls together the friends and neighbours, saying, Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus, I say unto you, there is joy before the angels ofGod for one repenting sinner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, A certain man had two sons;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the younger of them said to his father, Father, give to me the share of the property that falls [to me]. And he divided to them what he was possessed of.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after not many days the younger son gathering all together went away into a country a long way off, and there dissipated his property, living in debauchery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when he had spent all there arose a violent famine throughout that country, and he began to be in want.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he longed to fill his belly with the husks which the swine were eating; and no one gave to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And coming to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father's have abundance of bread, and I perish here by famine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will rise up and go to my father, and I will say to him, Father, I have sinned against heaven and before thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am no longer worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rose up and went to his own father. But while he was yet a long way off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and ran, and fell upon his neck, and covered him with kisses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the son said to him, Father, I have sinned against heaven and before thee; I am no longer worthy to be called thy son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the father said to his bondmen, Bring out the best robe and clothe him in [it], and put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and bring the fatted calf and kill it, and let us eat and make merry:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for this my son was dead and has come to life, was lost and has been found. And they began to make merry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his elder son was in the field; and as, coming [up], he drew nigh to the house, he heard music and dancing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called one of the servants, he inquired what these things might be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Thy brother is come, and thy father has killed the fatted calf because he has received him safe and well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he became angry and would not go in. And his father went out and besought him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said to his father, Behold, so many years I serve thee, and never have I transgressed a commandment of thine; and to me hast thou never given a kid that I might make merry with my friends:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but when this thy son, who has devoured thy substance with harlots, is come, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said to him, Child, thou art ever with me, and all that is mine is thine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But it was right to make merry and rejoice, because this thy brother was dead and has come to life again, and was lost and has been found.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said also to [his] disciples, There was a certain rich man who had a steward, and he was accused to him as wasting his goods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called him, he said to him, What [is] this that I hear of thee? give the reckoning of thy stewardship, for thou canst be no longer steward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the steward said within himself, What shall I do; for my lord is taking the stewardship from me? I am not able to dig; I am ashamed to beg.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I know what I will do, that when I shall have been removed from the stewardship I may be received into their houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called to [him] each one of the debtors of his own lord, he said to the first, How much owest thou to my lord?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, A hundred baths of oil. And he said to him, Take thy writing and sit down quickly and write fifty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he said to another, And thou, how much dost thou owe? And he said, A hundred cors of wheat. And he says to him, Take thy writing and write eighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lord praised the unrighteous steward because he had done prudently. For the sons of this world are, for their own generation, more prudent than the sons of light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I say to you, Make to yourselves friends with the mammon of unrighteousness, that when it fails ye may be received into the eternal tabernacles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is faithful in the least is faithful also in much; and he that is unrighteous in the least is unrighteous also in much.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who shall entrust to you the true?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if ye have not been faithful in that which is another's, who shall give to you your own?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and will love the other, or he will cleave to the one and despise the other. Ye cannot serveGod and mammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things, and mocked him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Ye are they who justify themselves before men, butGod knows your hearts; for what amongst men is highly thought of is an abomination beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The law and the prophets [were] until John: from that time the glad tidings of the kingdom ofGod are announced, and every one forces his way into it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But it is easier that the heaven and the earth should pass away than that one tittle of the law should fail.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every one who puts away his wife and marries another commits adultery; and every one that marries one put away from a husband commits adultery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there was a rich man and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, making good cheer in splendour every day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [there was] a poor man, by name Lazarus, [who] was laid at his gateway full of sores,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and desiring to be filled with the crumbs which fell from the table of the rich man; but the dogs also coming licked his sores.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass that the poor man died, and that he was carried away by the angels into the bosom of Abraham. And the rich man also died and was buried.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in hades lifting up his eyes, being in torments, he sees Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he crying out said, Father Abraham, have compassion on me, and send Lazarus that he may dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, for I am suffering in this flame.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Abraham said, Child, recollect that thou hast fully received thy good things in thy lifetime, and likewise Lazarus evil things. But now he is comforted here, and thou art in suffering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And besides all this, between us and you a great chasm is fixed, so that those who desire to pass hence to you cannot, nor do they who [desire to cross] from there pass over unto us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I beseech thee then, father, that thou wouldest send him to the house of my father,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I have five brothers, so that he may earnestly testify to them, that they also may not come to this place of torment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Abraham says to him, They have Moses and the prophets: let them hear them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said, Nay, father Abraham, but if one from the dead should go to them, they will repent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, If they hear not Moses and the prophets, not even if one rise from among [the] dead will they be persuaded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his disciples, It cannot be but that offences come, but woe [to him] by whom they come!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It would be [more] profitable for him if a millstone were hanged about his neck and he cast into the sea, than that he should be a snare to one of these little ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed to yourselves: if thy brother should sin, rebuke him; and if he should repent, forgive him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if he should sin against thee seven times in the day, and seven times should return to thee, saying, I repent, thou shalt forgive him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the apostles said to the Lord, Give more faith to us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Lord said, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard [seed], ye had said to this sycamine tree, Be thou rooted up, and be thou planted in the sea, and it would have obeyed you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But which of you [is there] who, having a bondman ploughing or shepherding, when he comes in out of the field, will say, Come and lie down immediately to table?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But will he not say to him, Prepare what I shall sup on, and gird thyself and serve me that I may eat and drink; and after that thou shalt eat and drink?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is he thankful to the bondman because he has done what was ordered? I judge not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus ye also, when ye shall have done all things that have been ordered you, say, We are unprofitable bondmen; we have done what it was our duty to do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he was going up to Jerusalem, that he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he entered into a certain village ten leprous men met him, who stood afar off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they lifted up [their] voice saying, Jesus, Master, have compassion on us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seeing [them] he said to them, Go, shew yourselves to the priests. And it came to pass as they were going they were cleansed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of them, seeing that he was cured, turned back, glorifyingGod with a loud voice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and fell on [his] face at his feet giving him thanks: and he was a Samaritan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, Were not the ten cleansed? but the nine, where [are they]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There have not been found to return and give glory toGod save this stranger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Rise up and go thy way: thy faith has made thee well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having been asked by the Pharisees, When is the kingdom ofGod coming? he answered them and said, The kingdom ofGod does not come with observation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor shall they say, Lo here, or, Lo there; for behold, the kingdom ofGod is in the midst of you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to the disciples, Days are coming, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and shall not see [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they will say to you, Lo here, or Lo there; go not, nor follow [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as the lightning shines which lightens from [one end] under heaven to [the other end] under heaven, thus shall the Son of man be in his day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But first he must suffer many things and be rejected of this generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as it took place in the days of Noe, thus also shall it be in the days of the Son of man:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they ate, they drank, they married, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark, and the flood came and destroyed all [of them];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in like manner as took place in the days of Lot: they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they builded;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but on the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire and sulphur from heaven, and destroyed all [of them]:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: after this [manner] shall it be in the day that the Son of man is revealed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In that day, he who shall be on the housetop, and his stuff in the house, let him not go down to take it away; and he that is in the field, let him likewise not return back.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember the wife of Lot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whosoever shall seek to save his life shall lose it, and whosoever shall lose it shall preserve it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I say to you, In that night there shall be two [men] upon one bed; one shall be seized and the other shall be let go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Two [women] shall be grinding together; the one shall be seized and the other shall be let go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Two men shall be in the field; the one shall be seized and the other let go.]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And answering they say to him, Where, Lord? And he said to them, Where the body [is], there the eagles will be gathered together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke also a parable to them to the purport that they should always pray and not faint,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, There was a judge in a city, not fearingGod and not respecting man:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there was a widow in that city, and she came to him, saying, Avenge me of mine adverse party.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he would not for a time; but afterwards he said within himself, If even I fear notGod and respect not man,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: at any rate because this widow annoys me I will avenge her, that she may not by perpetually coming completely harass me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord said, Hear what the unjust judge says.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And shall notGod at all avenge his elect, who cry to him day and night, and he bears long as to them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I say unto you that he will avenge them speedily. But when the Son of man comes, shall he indeed find faith on the earth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke also to some, who trusted in themselves that they were righteous and made nothing of all the rest [of men], this parable:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a tax-gatherer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Pharisee, standing, prayed thus to himself:God, I thank thee that I am not as the rest of men, rapacious, unjust, adulterers, or even as this tax-gatherer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I fast twice in the week, I tithe everything I gain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the tax-gatherer, standing afar off, would not lift up even his eyes to heaven, but smote upon his breast, saying, OGod, have compassion on me, the sinner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I say unto you, This [man] went down to his house justified rather than that [other]. For every one who exalts himself shall be humbled, and he that humbles himself shall be exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought to him also infants that he might touch them, but the disciples when they saw [it] rebuked them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus calling them to [him] said, Suffer little children to come to me, and do not forbid them, for of such is the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom ofGod as a little child shall in no wise enter therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain ruler asked him saying, Good teacher, having done what, shall I inherit eternal life?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to him, Why callest thou me good? There is none good but one,God.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou knowest the commandments: Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, All these things have I kept from my youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jesus had heard this, he said to him, One thing is lacking to thee yet: Sell all that thou hast and distribute to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in the heavens, and come, follow me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when he heard this he became very sorrowful, for he was very rich.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when Jesus saw that he became very sorrowful, he said, How difficultly shall those who have riches enter into the kingdom ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for it is easier for a camel to enter through a needle's eye than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those who heard it said, And who can be saved?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said, The things that are impossible with men are possible withGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter said, Behold, we have left all things and have followed thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Verily I say to you, There is no one who has left home, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdom ofGod's sake,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who shall not receive manifold more at this time, and in the coming age life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took the twelve to [him] and said to them, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and all things that are written of the Son of man by the prophets shall be accomplished;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he shall be delivered up to the nations, and shall be mocked, and insulted, and spit upon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they have scourged [him] they will kill him; and on the third day he will rise again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they understood nothing of these things. And this word was hidden from them, and they did not know what was said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he came into the neighbourhood of Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the wayside begging.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he heard the crowd passing, he inquired what this might be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they told him that Jesus the Nazaraean was passing by.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he called out saying, Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those [who were] going before rebuked him that he might be silent; but he cried out so much the more, Son of David, have mercy on me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be led to him. And when he drew nigh he asked him [saying],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What wilt thou that I shall do to thee? And he said, Lord, that I may see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, See: thy faith has healed thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately he saw, and followed him, glorifyingGod. And all the people when they saw [it] gave praise toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he entered and passed through Jericho.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, [there was] a man by name called Zacchaeus, and he was chief tax-gatherer, and he was rich.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sought to see Jesus who he was: and he could not for the crowd, because he was little in stature.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And running on before, he got up into a sycamore that he might see him, for he was going to pass that [way].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he came up to the place, Jesus looked up and saw him, and said to him, Zacchaeus, make haste and come down, for to-day I must remain in thy house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made haste and came down, and received him with joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all murmured when they saw [it], saying, He has turned in to lodge with a sinful man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord, Behold, Lord, the half of my goods I give to the poor, and if I have taken anything from any man by false accusation, I return [him] fourfold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, To-day salvation is come to this house, inasmuch as he also is a son of Abraham;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the Son of man has come to seek and to save that which is lost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as they were listening to these things, he added and spake a parable, because he was near to Jerusalem and they thought that the kingdom ofGod was about to be immediately manifested.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He said therefore, A certain high-born man went to a distant country to receive for himself a kingdom and return.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called his own ten bondmen, he gave to them ten minas, and said to them, Trade while I am coming.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But his citizens hated him, and sent an embassy after him, saying, We will not that this [man] should reign over us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on his arrival back again, having received the kingdom, that he desired these bondmen to whom he gave the money to be called to him, in order that he might know what every one had gained by trading.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first came up, saying, [My] Lord, thy mina has produced ten minas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Well [done], thou good bondman; because thou hast been faithful in that which is least, be thou in authority over ten cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the second came, saying, [My] Lord, thy mina has made five minas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said also to this one, And thou, be over five cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And another came, saying, [My] Lord, lo, [there is] thy mina, which I have kept laid up in a towel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I feared thee because thou art a harsh man: thou takest up what thou hast not laid down, and thou reapest what thou hast not sowed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says to him, Out of thy mouth will I judge thee, wicked bondman: thou knewest that I am a harsh man, taking up what I have not laid down and reaping what I have not sowed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And why didst thou not give my money to [the] bank; and I should have received it, at my coming, with interest?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to those that stood by, Take from him the mina and give [it] to him who has the ten minas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, Lord, he has ten minas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I say unto you, that to every one that has shall be given; but from him that has not, that even which he has shall be taken from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover those mine enemies, who would not [have] me to reign over them, bring them here and slay [them] before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said these things, he went on before, going up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he drew near to Bethphage and Bethany at the mountain called [the mount] of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Go into the village over against [you], in which ye will find, on entering it, a colt tied up, on which no [child] of man ever sat at any time: loose it and lead it [here].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if any one ask you, Why do ye loose [it]? thus shall ye say to him, Because the Lord has need of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that were sent, having gone their way, found as he had said to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were loosing the colt, its masters said to them, Why loose ye the colt?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Because the Lord has need of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they led it to Jesus; and having cast their own garments on the colt, they put Jesus on [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he went, they strewed their clothes in the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he drew near, already at the descent of the mount of Olives, all the multitude of the disciples began, rejoicing, to praiseGod with a loud voice for all the works of power which they had seen,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Blessed the King that comes in the name of [the] Lord: peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to him, Teacher, rebuke thy disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to them, I say unto you, If these shall be silent, the stones will cry out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he drew near, seeing the city, he wept over it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, If thou hadst known, even thou, even at least in this thy day, the things that are for thy peace: but now they are hid from thine eyes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall make a palisaded mound about thee, and shall close thee around, and keep thee in on every side,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children in thee; and shall not leave in thee a stone upon a stone: because thou knewest not the season of thy visitation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And entering into the temple, he began to cast out those that sold and bought in it,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying to them, It is written, My house is a house of prayer, but ye have made it a den of robbers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was teaching day by day in the temple: and the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to destroy him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and did not find what they could do, for all the people hung on him to hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on one of the days, as he was teaching the people in the temple, and announcing the glad tidings, the chief priests and the scribes with the elders came up,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and spoke to him saying, Tell us by what authority thou doest these things, or who is it who has given thee this authority?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to them, I also will ask you [one] thing, and tell me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The baptism of John, was it of heaven or of men?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, If we should say, Of heaven, he will say, Why have ye not believed him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if we should say, Of men, the whole people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they answered, they did not know whence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he began to speak to the people this parable: A man planted a vineyard and let it out to husbandmen, and left the country for a long time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the season he sent to the husbandmen a bondman, that they might give to him of the fruit of the vineyard; but the husbandmen, having beaten him, sent [him] away empty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again he sent another bondman; but they, having beaten him also, and cast insult upon him, sent [him] away empty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again he sent a third; and they, having wounded him also, cast [him] out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lord of the vineyard said, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: perhaps when they see him they will respect [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir; [come,] let us kill him, that the inheritance may become ours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having cast him forth out of the vineyard, they killed [him]. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do to them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will come and destroy those husbandmen, and will give the vineyard to others. And when they heard it they said, May it never be!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he looking at them said, What then is this that is written, The stone which they that builded rejected, this has become the corner-stone?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every one falling on this stone shall be broken, but on whomsoever it shall fall, it shall grind him to powder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests and the scribes sought the same hour to lay hands on him, and they feared the people; for they knew that he had spoken this parable of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having watched [him], they sent out suborned persons, pretending to be just men, that they might take hold of him in [his] language, so that they might deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they asked him saying, Teacher, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, and acceptest no [man's] person, but teachest with truth the way ofGod:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is it lawful for us to give tribute to Caesar, or not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But perceiving their deceit he said to them, Why do ye tempt me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shew me a denarius. Whose image and superscription has it? And answering they said, Caesar's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Pay therefore what is Caesar's to Caesar, and what isGod's toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were not able to take hold of him in [his] expressions before the people, and, wondering at his answer, they were silent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of the Sadducees, who deny that there is any resurrection, coming up [to him],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: demanded of him saying, Teacher, Moses wrote to us, If any one's brother, who has a wife, die, and he die childless, his brother shall take the wife and raise up seed to his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There were then seven brethren: and the first, having taken a wife, died childless;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the second [took the woman, and he died childless];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the third took her: and in like manner also the seven left no children and died;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and last of all the woman also died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the resurrection therefore of which of them does she become wife, for the seven had her as wife?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, The sons of this world marry and are given in marriage,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but they who are counted worthy to have part in that world, and the resurrection from among [the] dead, neither marry nor are given in marriage;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for neither can they die any more, for they are equal to angels, and are sons ofGod, being sons of the resurrection.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that the dead rise, even Moses shewed in [the section of] the bush, when he called [the] Lord theGod of Abraham and theGod of Isaac and theGod of Jacob;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he is notGod of [the] dead but of [the] living; for all live for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of the scribes answering said, Teacher, thou hast well spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they did not dare any more to ask him anything.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, How do they say that the Christ is David's son,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and David himself says in the book of Psalms, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: until I put thine enemies [as] footstool of thy feet?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: David therefore calls him Lord, and how is he his son?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, as all the people were listening, he said to his disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Beware of the scribes, who like to walk about in long robes, and who love salutations in the market-places, and first seats in the synagogues, and first places at suppers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who devour the houses of widows, and as a pretext make long prayers. These shall receive a severer judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he looked up and saw the rich casting their gifts into the treasury;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he saw also a certain poor widow casting therein two mites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Verily I say unto you, that this poor widow has cast in more than all;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for all these out of their abundance have cast into the gifts [ofGod]; but she out of her need has cast in all the living which she had.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as some spoke of the temple, that it was adorned with goodly stones and consecrated offerings, he said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As to] these things which ye are beholding, days are coming in which there shall not be left stone upon stone which shall not be thrown down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they asked him saying, Teacher, when then shall these things be; and what [is] the sign when these things are going to take place?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, See that ye be not led astray, for many shall come in my name, saying, I am [he], and the time is drawn nigh: go ye not [therefore] after them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when ye shall hear of wars and tumults, be not terrified, for these things must first take place, but the end is not immediately.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he said to them, Nation shall rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: there shall be both great earthquakes in different places, and famines and pestilences; and there shall be fearful sights and great signs from heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But before all these things they shall lay their hands upon you and persecute you, delivering [you] up to synagogues and prisons, bringing [you] before kings and governors on account of my name;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but it shall turn out to you for a testimony.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Settle therefore in your hearts not to meditate beforehand [your] defence,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your opposers shall not be able to reply to or resist.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye will be delivered up even by parents and brethren and relations and friends, and they shall put to death [some] from among you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye will be hated of all for my name's sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a hair of your head shall in no wise perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
