translations.text: By your patient endurance gain your souls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when ye see Jerusalem encompassed with armies, then know that its desolation is drawn nigh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then let those who are in Judaea flee to the mountains, and those who are in the midst of it depart out, and those who are in the country not enter into it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for these are days of avenging, that all the things that are written may be accomplished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But woe to them that are with child and to them who give suck in those days, for there shall be great distress upon the land and wrath upon this people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and be led captive into all the nations; and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of [the] nations until [the] times of [the] nations be fulfilled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there shall be signs in sun and moon and stars, and upon the earth distress of nations in perplexity [at] the roar of the sea and rolling waves,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: men ready to die through fear and expectation of what is coming on the habitable earth, for the powers of the heavens shall be shaken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when these things begin to come to pass, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption draws nigh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke a parable to them: Behold the fig-tree and all the trees;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when they already sprout, ye know of your own selves, [on] looking [at them], that already the summer is near.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So also ye, when ye see these things take place, know that the kingdom ofGod is near.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say unto you, that this generation shall in no wise pass away until all come to pass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heaven and the earth shall pass away, but my words shall in no wise pass away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But take heed to yourselves lest possibly your hearts be laden with surfeiting and drinking and cares of life, and that day come upon you suddenly unawares;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for as a snare shall it come upon all them that dwell upon the face of the whole earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Watch therefore, praying at every season, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things which are about to come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by day he was teaching in the temple, and by night, going out, he remained abroad on the mountain called [the mount] of Olives;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the feast of unleavened bread, which [is] called the passover, drew nigh,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Satan entered into Judas, who was surnamed Iscariote, being of the number of the twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went away and spoke with the chief priests and captains as to how he should deliver him up to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were rejoiced, and agreed to give him money.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to an agreement to do it, and sought an opportunity to deliver him up to them away from the crowd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the day of unleavened bread came, in which the passover was to be killed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent Peter and John, saying, Go and prepare the passover for us, that we may eat [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they said to him, Where wilt thou that we prepare [it]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Behold, as ye enter into the city a man will meet you, carrying an earthen pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he goes in;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye shall say to the master of the house, The Teacher says to thee, Where is the guest-chamber where I may eat the passover with my disciples?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he will shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having gone they found it as he had said to them; and they prepared the passover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the hour was come, he placed himself at table, and the [twelve] apostles with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I say unto you, that I will not eat any more at all of it until it be fulfilled in the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having received a cup, when he had given thanks he said, Take this and divide it among yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I say unto you, that I will not drink at all of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom ofGod come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken a loaf, when he had given thanks, he broke [it], and gave [it] to them, saying, This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In like manner also the cup, after having supped, saying, This cup [is] the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover, behold, the hand of him that delivers me up [is] with me on the table;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the Son of man indeed goes as it is determined, but woe unto that man by whom he is delivered up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they began to question together among themselves who then it could be of them who was about to do this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was also a strife among them which of them should be held to be [the] greatest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, The kings of the nations rule over them, and they that exercise authority over them are called benefactors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye [shall] not [be] thus; but let the greater among you be as the younger, and the leader as he that serves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For which [is] greater, he that is at table or he that serves? [Is] not he that is at table? But I am in the midst of you as the one that serves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye are they who have persevered with me in my temptations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I appoint unto you, as my Father has appointed unto me, a kingdom,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, Satan has demanded to have you, to sift [you] as wheat;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I have besought for thee that thy faith fail not; and thou, when once thou hast been restored, confirm thy brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Lord, with thee I am ready to go both to prison and to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I tell thee, Peter, [the] cock shall not crow to-day before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, When I sent you without purse and scrip and sandals, did ye lack anything? And they said, Nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He said therefore to them, But now he that has a purse let him take [it], in like manner also a scrip, and he that has none let him sell his garment and buy a sword;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned with [the] lawless: for also the things concerning me have an end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Lord, behold here are two swords. And he said to them, It is enough.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going forth he went according to his custom to the mount of Olives, and the disciples also followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was at the place he said to them, Pray that ye enter not into temptation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was withdrawn from them about a stone's throw, and having knelt down he prayed,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Father, if thou wilt remove this cup from me: — but then, not my will, but thine be done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And an angel appeared to him from heaven strengthening him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And being in conflict he prayed more intently. And his sweat became as great drops of blood, falling down upon the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And rising up from his prayer, coming to the disciples, he found them sleeping from grief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Why sleep ye? rise up and pray that ye enter not into temptation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As he was yet speaking, behold, a crowd, and he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, went on before them, and drew near to Jesus to kiss him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, Judas, deliverest thou up the Son of man with a kiss?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they who were around him, seeing what was going to follow, said [to him], Lord, shall we smite with [the] sword?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain one from among them smote the bondman of the high priest and took off his right ear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, Suffer thus far; and having touched his ear, he healed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to the chief priests and captains of the temple and elders, who were come against him, Have ye come out as against a robber with swords and sticks?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I was day by day with you in the temple ye did not stretch out your hands against me; but this is your hour and the power of darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having laid hold on him, they led him [away], and they led [him] into the house of the high priest. And Peter followed afar off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they having lit a fire in the midst of the court and sat down together, Peter sat among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain maid, having seen him sitting by the light, and having fixed her eyes upon him, said, And this [man] was with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he denied [him], saying, Woman, I do not know him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after a short time another seeing him said, And thou art of them. But Peter said, Man, I am not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after the lapse of about one hour another stoutly maintained it, saying, In truth this [man] also was with him, for also he is a Galilaean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter said, Man, I know not what thou sayest. And immediately, while he was yet speaking, [the] cock crew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord, turning round, looked at Peter; and Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he said to him, Before [the] cock crow thou shalt deny me thrice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter, going forth without, wept bitterly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men who held him mocked him, beating [him];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and covering him up, asked him saying, Prophesy, who is it that struck thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said many other injurious things to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was day, the elderhood of the people, both [the] chief priests and scribes, were gathered together, and led him into their council, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou art the Christ, tell us. And he said to them, If I tell you, ye will not at all believe;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if I should ask [you], ye would not answer me at all, nor let me go;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but henceforth shall the Son of man be sitting on the right hand of the power ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they all said, Thou then art the Son ofGod? And he said to them, Ye say that I am.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, What need have we any more of witness, for we have heard ourselves out of his mouth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the whole multitude of them, rising up, led him to Pilate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they began to accuse him, saying, We have found this [man] perverting our nation, and forbidding to give tribute to Caesar, saying that he himself is Christ, a king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate demanded of him saying, Art thou the king of the Jews? And he answering him said, Thou sayest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no guilt in this man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they insisted, saying, He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judaea, beginning from Galilee even on to here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Pilate, having heard Galilee [named], demanded if the man were a Galilaean;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having learned that he was of Herod's jurisdiction, remitted him to Herod, who himself also was at Jerusalem in those days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Herod saw Jesus he greatly rejoiced, for he had been a long while desirous of seeing him, because of hearing many things concerning him, and he hoped to see some sign done by him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he questioned him in many words, but he answered him nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests and the scribes stood and accused him violently.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Herod with his troops having set him at nought and mocked him, having put a splendid robe upon him, sent him back to Pilate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate and Herod became friends with one another the same day, for they had been at enmity before between themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate, having called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: said to them, Ye have brought to me this man as turning away the people [to rebellion], and behold, I, having examined him before you, have found nothing criminal in this man as to the things of which ye accuse him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor Herod either, for I remitted you to him, and behold, nothing worthy of death is done by him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having chastised him therefore, I will release him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (Now he was obliged to release one for them at the feast.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they cried out in a mass saying, Away with this [man] and release Barabbas to us;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who was one who, for a certain tumult which had taken place in the city, and [for] murder, had been cast into prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate therefore, desirous to release Jesus, again addressed [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they cried out in reply saying, Crucify, crucify him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said the third time to them, What evil then has this [man] done? I have found no cause of death in him: I will chastise him therefore and release him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they were urgent with loud voices, begging that he might be crucified. And their voices [and those of the chief priests] prevailed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate adjudged that what they begged should take place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he released him who, for tumult and murder, had been cast into prison, whom they begged for, and Jesus he delivered up to their will.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they led him away, they laid hold on a certain Simon, a Cyrenian, coming from the field, and put the cross upon him to bear it behind Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a great multitude of the people, and of women who wailed and lamented him, followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus turning round to them said, Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep over me, but weep over yourselves and over your children;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for behold, days are coming in which they will say, Blessed [are] the barren, and wombs that have not borne, and breasts that have not given suck.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall upon us; and to the hills, Cover us:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for if these things are done in the green tree, what shall take place in the dry?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now two others also, malefactors, were led with him to be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they came to the place which is called Skull, there they crucified him, and the malefactors, one on the right hand, the other on the left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do. And, parting out his garments, they cast lots.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people stood beholding, and the rulers also [with them] sneered, saying, He has saved others; let him save himself if this is the Christ, the chosen one ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the soldiers also made game of him, coming up offering him vinegar,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and saying, If thou be the king of the Jews, save thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was also an inscription [written] over him in Greek, and Roman, and Hebrew letters: This is the King of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now one of the malefactors who had been hanged spoke insultingly to him, saying, Art not thou the Christ? save thyself and us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the other answering rebuked him, saying, Dost thou too not fearGod, thou that art under the same judgment?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and we indeed justly, for we receive the just recompense of what we have done; but this [man] has done nothing amiss.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to Jesus, Remember me, [Lord,] when thou comest in thy kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, Verily I say to thee, To-day shalt thou be with me in paradise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was about [the] sixth hour, and there came darkness over the whole land until [the] ninth hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple rent in the midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, having cried with a loud voice, said, Father, into thy hands I commit my spirit. And having said this, he expired.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the centurion, seeing what took place, glorifiedGod, saying, In very deed this man was just.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the crowds who had come together to that sight, having seen the things that took place, returned, beating [their] breasts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all those who knew him stood afar off, the women also who had followed him from Galilee, beholding these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, a man named Joseph, who was a councillor, a good man and a just

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (this [man] had not assented to their counsel and deed), of Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who also waited, [himself also,] for the kingdom ofGod

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — he having gone to Pilate begged the body of Jesus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having taken it down, wrapped it in fine linen and placed him in a tomb hewn in the rock, where no one had ever been laid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was preparation day, and [the] sabbath twilight was coming on.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And women, who had come along with him out of Galilee, having followed, saw the sepulchre and how his body was placed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having returned they prepared aromatic spices and ointments, and remained quiet on the sabbath, according to the commandment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But on the morrow of the sabbath, very early indeed in the morning, they came to the tomb, bringing the aromatic spices which they had prepared.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had entered they found not the body of the Lord Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as they were in perplexity about it, that behold, two men suddenly stood by them in shining raiment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were filled with fear and bowed their faces to the ground, they said to them, Why seek ye the living one among the dead?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spoke to you, being yet in Galilee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, The Son of man must be delivered up into the hands of sinners, and be crucified, and rise the third day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they remembered his words;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, returning from the sepulchre, related all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now it was Mary of Magdala, and Johanna, and Mary the [mother] of James, and the others with them, who told these things to the apostles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their words appeared in their eyes as an idle tale, and they disbelieved them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter, rising up, ran to the sepulchre, and stooping down he sees the linen clothes lying there alone, and went away home, wondering at what had happened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, two of them were going on the same day to a village distant sixty stadia from Jerusalem, called Emmaus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they conversed with one another about all these things which had taken place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as they conversed and reasoned, that Jesus himself drawing nigh, went with them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but their eyes were holden so as not to know him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, What discourses are these which pass between you as ye walk, and are downcast?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one [of them], named Cleopas, answering said to him, Thou sojournest alone in Jerusalem, and dost not know what has taken place in it in these days?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, What things? And they said to him, The things concerning Jesus the Nazaraean, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word beforeGod and all the people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him up to [the] judgment of death and crucified him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we had hoped that he was [the one] who is about to redeem Israel. But then, besides all these things, it is now, to-day, the third day since these things took place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And withal, certain women from amongst us astonished us, having been very early at the sepulchre,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, not having found his body, came, saying that they also had seen a vision of angels, who say that he is living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of those with us went to the sepulchre, and found it so, as the women also had said, but him they saw not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, O senseless and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ought not the Christ to have suffered these things and to enter into his glory?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having begun from Moses and from all the prophets, he interpreted to them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they drew near to the village where they were going, and he made as though he would go farther.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they constrained him, saying, Stay with us, for it is toward evening and the day is declining. And he entered in to stay with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he was at table with them, having taken the bread, he blessed, and having broken it, gave it to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their eyes were opened, and they recognised him. And he disappeared from them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to one another, Was not our heart burning in us as he spoke to us on the way, [and] as he opened the scriptures to us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And rising up the same hour, they returned to Jerusalem. And they found the eleven, and those with them, gathered together,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, The Lord is indeed risen and has appeared to Simon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they related what [had happened] on the way, and how he was made known to them in the breaking of bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were saying these things, he himself stood in their midst, and says to them, Peace [be] unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, being confounded and being frightened, supposed they beheld a spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Why are ye troubled? and why are thoughts rising in your hearts?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself. Handle me and see, for a spirit has not flesh and bones as ye see me having.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said this he shewed them his hands and his feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But while they yet did not believe for joy, and were wondering, he said to them, Have ye anything here to eat?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave him part of a broiled fish and of a honeycomb;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he took it and ate before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, These [are] the words which I spoke to you while I was yet with you, that all that is written concerning me in the law of Moses and prophets and psalms must be fulfilled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he opened their understanding to understand the scriptures,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to them, Thus it is written, and thus it behoved the Christ to suffer, and to rise from among the dead the third day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name to all the nations beginning at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye are witnesses of these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you; but do ye remain in the city till ye be clothed with power from on high.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he led them out as far as Bethany, and having lifted up his hands, he blessed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he was blessing them, he was separated from them and was carried up into heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they, having done him homage, returned to Jerusalem with great joy,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and were continually in the temple praising and blessingGod.
